one_line_description: GTM data enrichment and workflow platform that orchestrates 150+ data providers in spreadsheet-style tables.
auth_summary: No general public API auth scheme is documented. Inbound webhooks support an optional auth token header; the Enterprise-only People & Company lookup API and the MCP integration are key/account-based (MCP connects via a guided login in Claude/ChatGPT connectors).
access_notes: Clay itself is self-serve SaaS, but per Clay's own docs/community answers it 'doesn't offer traditional API endpoints': developers get data in via self-serve webhooks and out via the in-table HTTP API action, while the direct People & Company enrichment API is restricted to Enterprise customers.
api_surface_summary: Generally available surface is webhook ingestion into tables (50k submissions per webhook) plus an outbound HTTP API action from tables; a proper REST lookup API (people/company) exists only for Enterprise plans.
main_blocker: No general-purpose public API — programmatic enrichment access is gated to Enterprise; everything else is webhook-in/HTTP-out around Clay tables.
agent_notes: Fetched clay.com/guides/clay-mcp: Clay has an official MCP exposing 150+ providers and workflows inside ChatGPT/Claude/Codex (500 free credits on first connect), which is arguably the sanctioned agent path in lieu of a public API. Caution: search results also surfaced an unrelated 'Clay' personal CRM (clay.earth, mcp.clay.earth, clay-inc/clay-mcp on GitHub) — do not conflate the two. Enterprise API details (auth header, endpoints) are not publicly documented.
